data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T10 (Infrastructure / IaC overlay): CLEAN, no findings.

AFFIRMATIONS:
- T10 is an OVERLAY that applies only when a plan provisions/configures infrastructure (Terraform/CloudFormation/CDK/Pulumi/Ansible, K8s/Helm, cloud resources, IAM). This plan ships a Python CLI/library feature (the plan-review gate) and provisions NO infrastructure — so T10 correctly resolves to not-applicable → PASS, per its own ANTI-FP rule ("not-applicable for non-infra tickets"). The plan explicitly leans on this for libraries/CLIs ("the core anti-FP for libraries/CLIs like rebar itself").
- The plan's DESIGN of T10 is internally consistent and experiment-grounded: T10 is LLM-routed for plan review (the "FINALIZE CORRECTION (E4-confirmed)" section grounds this — deterministic IaC keywords over-fired on the non-DSO corpus, LLM router fired 0 false), classified AGENT-tier (an IaC verdict depends on the actual `.tf`; the agent found a real committed secret a single-turn pass missed — `criteria_v8.json` `_tier_note`, line 1178), advisory by default (`block_threshold` 0.95), with the full seven binary sub-checks (state/IAM/idempotency/blast-radius/secrets/cost/observability) carried in `criteria_v8.json` (lines 1144-1172) and a `suppress_types:["bug"]` rule consistent with the epic's bug carve-out.

No `- [ ]` checklist findings for T10.
